devdoc.details: Implements the Q402 protocol: sign-to-pay gasless ERC-20 transfers.  Architecture (EIP-7702 flow):  ┌──────────┐  sign EIP-712   ┌─────────────────────────────┐  │  User    │ ───────────────▶ │  Q402PaymentImplementation  │  │  (EOA)   │                  │  (deployed implementation)  │  └──────────┘                  └─────────────────────────────┘       │                                    │       │  EIP-7702 delegation               │ verifies sig       ▼                                    │ transfers token  ┌──────────┐  Type-0x04 tx   ┌───────────▼─────────────────┐  │Facilitator│ ──────────────▶ │   User EOA (delegated code) │  │(gas sponsor)│               │   IERC20.transfer(...)      │  └──────────┘                  └─────────────────────────────┘  EIP-712 domain name : "Q402 Arbitrum"  Witness type        : TransferAuthorization  Chain ID (Mainnet)  : 42161  Native gas token    : ETH  Arbitrum-specific notes:  - EIP-7702 fully supported on Arbitrum One since ArbOS 40 "Callisto",    further refined by ArbOS 51 "Dia" (activated 2026-01-08) which    aligned precompile delegation behavior with the EIP-7702 spec.    Set-code transactions (type 0x04) are accepted by the Arbitrum    sequencer and finalized through the standard Nitro stack — no    chain-specific handling on the relayer side.  - Arbitrum Nitro is byte-level EVM-equivalent: the DOMAIN_TYPEHASH /    TRANSFER_AUTHORIZATION_TYPEHASH and ecrecover are bit-identical to    Ethereum mainnet, so a signature produced for "Q402 Arbitrum"    verifies the same way the Avalanche / BNB / Scroll variants verify    on their respective chains.  - USDC on Arbitrum One is native Circle USDC (CCTP, 0xaf88...e5831).    We intentionally do NOT support the legacy bridged USDC.e variant    (0xFF970A61...5CC8) to avoid the bridged/native confusion that    Mantle's USDT migration created. USDT is Tether's canonical    Arbitrum deployment — both are standard 6-decimal ERC-20s that    accept IERC20.transfer from a delegated EOA.  - Arbitrum is an Optimistic Rollup L2: practical confirmation is    sub-second via the sequencer, hard finality (L1 confirmation)    takes ~7 days through the standard challenge window. Q402's relay    treats sequencer confirmation as final, matching the UX expected    by every Arbitrum-native dApp.

transferFromWithAuthorization(address,ad.details: Fallback for chains without EIP-7702 support.

transferWithAuthorization(address,addres.details: Called by the Facilitator (who pays ETH gas) after setting the      EIP-7702 delegation on the user's EOA.      Under EIP-7702: `address(this)` == user's EOA, so IERC20.transfer()      sends tokens directly from the user's balance without allowance.
